2605.12476.title: Routers Learn the Geometry of Their Experts: Geometric Coupling in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
2605.12476.paper_summary: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models enable scaling language models efficiently, but training them remains challenging, as routing can collapse onto few experts and auxiliary load-balancing losses can reduce specialization. Motivated by these hurdles, we study how routing decisions in SMoEs are formed mechanistically. First, we reveal a geometric coupling between routers and their corresponding experts. For a given token, the router weights for the selected expert and the expert weights processing it receive gradients along the same input direction, differing only in scalar coefficients. Thus, matched router--expert directions accumulate the same routed token history. This theoretical coupling also appears empirically in routing dynamics. In a $1$B SMoE trained from scratch, higher router scores predict stronger expert neuron activations, showing that routing decisions are mirrored inside the selected expert. Next, we analyze the effects of auxiliary load balancing on the router--expert geometric coupling, showing that such losses break this structure by spreading input-directed gradients across router weights, making distinct router directions nearly three times more similar to each other. Last, we demonstrate the centrality of geometric coupling for effective routing with a parameter-free online K-Means router, in which each expert maintains a running average of the hidden states routed to it and tokens are assigned based on cosine similarity. Compared with auxiliary-loss and loss-free balancing, this router achieves the lowest load imbalance with only a modest perplexity increase, indicating that geometric coupling captures a substantial part of what the router learns. Overall, our results explain how routers form assignment geometry that supports an effective division of labor.
2605.12476.paper_summary_zh: 稀疏專家混合模型（SMoE）能夠有效擴展語言模型，但訓練它們仍然具有挑戰性，因為路由可能會集中在少數專家上，而輔助負載平衡損失可能會降低專業化。受到這些障礙的啟發，我們研究了SMoE中路由決策是如何機械性地形成的。首先，我們揭示了路由器與其對應專家之間的幾何耦合。對於給定的標記，所選專家的路由器權重和處理它的專家權重沿著相同的輸入方向接收梯度，僅在標量係數上有所不同。因此，匹配的路由器-專家方向累積相同的路由標記歷史。這種理論耦合在路由動態中也在實證中出現。在一個從零開始訓練的$1$B SMoE中，更高的路由器分數預測更強的專家神經元激活，顯示路由決策在所選專家內部是相互映射的。接下來，我們分析了輔助負載平衡對路由器-專家幾何耦合的影響，顯示這些損失通過將輸入導向的梯度分散到路由器權重上來破壞這一結構，使得不同的路由器方向彼此之間幾乎三倍相似。最後，我們展示了幾何耦合對於有效路由的中心性，使用一個無參數的在線K均值路由器，其中每個專家維護一個路由到它的隱藏狀態的運行平均，並根據余弦相似度分配標記。與輔助損失和無損失平衡相比，這個路由器在僅略微增加困惑度的情況下實現了最低的負載不平衡，表明幾何耦合捕捉了路由器學習的實質部分。總體而言，我們的結果解釋了路由器如何形成支持有效分工的分配幾何。
2605.12476.author: Sagi Ahrac et.al.
2605.12476.authors: Sagi Ahrac, Noya Hochwald, Mor Geva
